_comment: Example of a completed, locked lens JSON. Use as reference for lens_dialogue.py output format.
_note: Synthetic example — all content is illustrative.
theoretical_orientation: Constructivist phenomenological approach, informed by Merleau-Ponty's embodied cognition. I understand experience as always already shaped by the body, context, and relational field. I attend to what is said and how it is said — rhythm, hesitation, and qualification carry meaning for me.
clinical_orientation: Seven years as an integrative therapist working primarily with adults experiencing depression and relational difficulty. I notice I am drawn to rupture and repair moments, and to how clients narrate causality — who or what they make responsible for their suffering.

primary_hypotheses.hypothesis: Participants will describe a tension between wanting to be understood and expecting not to be
Help-seeking language will be hedged or qualified in ways that indicate shame
The therapeutic relationship will be described through metaphors of proximity and distance
primary_hypotheses.posthoc_flag: This hypothesis emerged during Pass 1 review and was not in the pre-registration. Flagged as post-hoc.

explicit_exclusions: Attribution of theoretical constructs not named by participants
Comparison between participants at this stage
vulnerable_groups_power_dynamics: Yes — clients in an ongoing therapeutic relationship with the researcher-therapist. Power differential is significant. Participants may present experiences as more positive than felt to manage the relationship.
expected_participant_indirection: Expect significant hedging, qualification, and displacement of distress onto third parties or past events rather than direct expression in the session context.
pass1_surprises: I was struck by how often participants named specific sensory details — temperature, texture, sound — when describing moments of connection or disconnection. I had not anticipated this as a prominent feature and had not included embodied sensation in my pre-registered hypotheses. This feels significant and I want to attend to it in Pass 2 without over-interpreting from my embodied theory background.
posthoc_hypothesis_flags: Proximity/distance metaphor hypothesis was not in pre-registration — flagged as post-hoc
lens_summary: I bring a constructivist phenomenological frame, attentiveness to relational dynamics and embodied experience, and clinical sensitivity to shame and hedging in help-seeking language. I will attend to sensory and embodied description as an emergent feature. I will hold the post-hoc proximity/distance hypothesis lightly and mark it as interpretive proposition if evidential support is weak.
